framework: IMDRF Software as a Medical Device (SaMD): Possible Framework for Risk Categorization

significance.description: Significance of information provided by the SaMD to the healthcare decision

values.treat_diagnose: Treat or diagnose - information used to take immediate or near-term action
values.drive: Drive clinical management - information used to aid in treatment, diagnosis, triage or to guide next diagnostics
values.inform: Inform clinical management - information will not trigger immediate or near-term action

healthcare_situation.description: State of the healthcare situation or condition

values.critical: Critical situation or condition - accurate/timely action is vital to avoid death, long-term disability or serious deterioration
values.serious: Serious situation or condition - accurate diagnosis/treatment is important but not always critical
values.non_serious: Non-serious situation or condition - mild/slowly progressing without lasting consequences

I.description: Lowest impact on patient or public health.

II.description: Low-moderate impact on the patient or public health.

III.description: Moderate-high impact; supports decisions in serious or critical situations.
requirements: Comprehensive clinical validation with representative populations
Predetermined Change Control Plan (PCCP) for adaptive algorithms

IV.description: Highest impact; used to treat or diagnose in critical situations.
requirements: Independent clinical validation and, where applicable, clinical investigation
Rigorous bias and equity assessment across protected subgroups
Predetermined Change Control Plan (PCCP) with regulatory pre-specification
Real-world performance monitoring with rollback and incident reporting
Human factors / usability validation for clinical decision support
